impact:
Discovered during T-017. The community-sourced wormholes.json covers 2,568 of the SDE's 2,604 J-space systems; 37 are unrepresented in system_statics (and 13 in both statics + effects). The gap is concentrated in shattered class-13 systems (~32 J-numbered) and the 5 named drifter complexes (Azdaja Redoubt, Conflux Eyrie, Liberated Barbican, Sanctified Vidette, Sentinel MZ) — exactly the systems WHDBX-derived datasets don't track.

User impact today: NONE. `wormhole_positions.json` includes all 2,604 systems (positions are SDE-driven, not community-driven), so /map renders every J-system correctly. The wormhole system detail route calls `_wh_data.get('system_statics', {}).get(system_name, [])` and `_wh_data.get('system_effects', {}).get(system_name)` — missing keys silently fall back to empty list / None and the partial just renders without a statics block. The kills route doesn't touch system_statics at all.

This is not data we can fill from SDE — statics are per-system community-curated knowledge that EVE doesn't expose. Realistic remediation is either pulling fresher community data (anoikis.info / fuzzwork export / WHDBX successor) on a recurring basis, or accepting the gap.

Filing as low severity, primarily as documentation. The position pipeline was verified current against today's SDE during T-017 (no diff).
resolution:
Closed as **UX-improvement-with-documentation-of-the-gap**. The data gap itself remains — there is no community source that covers these 37 systems and they aren't fillable from the SDE — but the UI now surfaces the absence explicitly instead of silently omitting the section.

**Verification of the gap (2026-05-21):** Diffed live SDE J-systems (2,604) vs `app/data/wormholes.json:system_statics` (2,568). Missing exactly 37 systems matching the original ISS-014 finding:
- 5 named drifter complexes: Azdaja Redoubt (class 18), Conflux Eyrie (17), Liberated Barbican (15), Sanctified Vidette (16), Sentinel MZ (14).
- 32 J-numbered systems with `wormhole_class_id = NULL` in the SDE (shattered/uncatalogued).

**What changed:**

- `app/routes/wormholes.py:wormhole_system` — added a `statics_known` boolean to the response context: true when the system name is present as a key in `system_statics` (even with an empty list), false when the key is absent. Distinguishes "data not curated for this system" from "system has no statics".
- `app/templates/wormhole_system.html` — when `statics` is empty AND `statics_known` is false, render an italicised note in the Statics row: *"Not catalogued — community data does not cover this system"*. When `statics_known` is true with an empty list (would be an unusual case — none today), the section stays hidden, matching prior behavior.

**What was rejected and why:**

- Filling the 37 entries with explicit empty-list values in wormholes.json — same runtime behavior as the current `.get(name, [])` fallback, no signal gained.
- Pulling fresher community data (anoikis.info / WHDBX successor) — those datasets are derived from WHDBX and explicitly don't track these systems, so a pull wouldn't close the gap. Documented in the original ISS-014 finding.

**Net effect:** A user landing on `/wormholes/system/Sentinel%20MZ` (or any of the 36 others) now sees an explicit "Not catalogued" note rather than wondering why the page is missing a Statics row. Same content for the 2,568 systems that ARE in the map.

Commit: pending deploy.
title: 37 J-systems known to SDE but absent from wormholes.json:system_statics
